title: Semi Truck Accident in Lubbock, Texas: What to Do (2026)

linkedin:
If you or a client has been involved in a semi truck accident in Lubbock, Texas, the legal landscape is significantly more complex than a standard auto collision. FMCSA federal regulations, hours-of-service logs, electronic black box data, and multiple potentially liable parties — the trucking company, driver, cargo loader, and maintenance contractor — all come into play immediately after a crash.

Critically, evidence like electronic logging device (ELD) data and dashcam footage can be overwritten or destroyed within days. Preservation letters and spoliation notices need to go out fast.

WreckMatch connects injured Texans with attorneys from our network of 800+ law firms nationwide who specialize in commercial truck litigation. Our matching process is free, fast, and built specifically for cases where the stakes are high and timing is everything.

reddit_title: Hit by a semi truck in Lubbock, TX — what should I actually be doing right now to protect my case?
reddit_body:
If you were just in a semi truck accident in Lubbock, the most important thing to understand is that these cases move on a much tighter timeline than regular car crashes. Electronic logging device data, dashcam footage, and black box telemetry from the truck can be overwritten or legally destroyed in as little as 72 hours. A preservation letter needs to go to the trucking company as soon as possible, which is one of the first things a qualified attorney will do.

On the liability side, it is rarely just the driver. The trucking company, the cargo loading company, the vehicle maintenance contractor, and even the truck manufacturer can all potentially share fault. Texas follows a modified comparative fault rule, so how liability is allocated really matters for your recovery. FMCSA regulations add another layer that most general practice attorneys are not deeply familiar with.

For finding an attorney who actually specializes in commercial truck litigation rather than a general personal injury firm, a service like wreckmatch.com can match you with lawyers from a large network who focus specifically on truck accident cases. Whatever you do, avoid giving recorded statements to the trucking company's insurer before you have representation — they are working against your interests from the first call.
